english: ternal world, (yet cf. no. 2); spyód-yul (spyod yul), q.v. — 2. the object or objects of perception by means of the senses; p̱yíi yul drug (phyi'i yul drug) the provinces of the six senses, viz. forms (the external appearances of bodies), sounds etc. Mil.; so prob. also: yúl-rnams-la lóṅs-spyod-par rmóṅs-te (yul rnams la longs spyod par rmongs te) Wdṅ. dead to sensual pleasures; yul mi ꜝdzín-pa (yul mi 'dzin pa), or gźán-du ꜝdzín-pa (gzhan du 'dzin pa) Thgy. to perceive things either not at all, or not correctly; brjód-pai yúl-las ꜝdás-pa (brjod pa'i yul las 'das pa) is stated to imply: exceeding the limits of speech, unspeakable, unutterable; bsám-byai yúl-las ꜝdás-pa (bsam bya'i yul las 'das pa) = bsám-gyis mi kyáb-pa (bsam gyis mi khyab pa) frq. unimaginable, inconceivable, which term, however, does not seem to be fully adequate; also Was. (311) translates yul (yul) with object; cf. gnas (gnas), 5. — 3. weather, or rather in a more gen. sense, climatic state of a country, and condition of the beings in it, v. below yul-ṅán (yul ngan), yul-bzáṅ (yul bzang). Comp. and deriv. yul-ḵáms (yul khams) kingdom, e.g. of Nepal, China, Glr. — yul-ꜝkór (yul 'khor) country, province Glr. — yul-gru (yul gru) id. Glr. — yul-dgón (yul dgon) v. above. — yul-ṅán (yul ngan) C. tempest, yul-ṅán-gyi tsúb-ma (yul ngan gyi tsub ma) the turmoil of the tempest Glr.; also public calamities, such as famine, murrain etc., Glr. — yúl-c̀an (yul can) 1. suited, proper, being in its place, fulfilling its purpose, Cs. (?) 2. that which is treated 'objectively' Was. 311, cf. no. 2 above. — yúl-c̀os (yul chos) characteristic properties, manners etc. of a country. — yul-ljóṅs (yul ljongs) district, tract of country. — *yul tum-túm* (*yul tum tum*) Ld. the separate villages of a whole cluster bearing one common name. — yul-sdé (yul sde) 1. district C., W. 2. village magistrate. — yúl-pa (yul pa) inhabitant, native, gaṅ yúl-pa yin (gang yul pa yin) whence are you? what is your country? — citizen, burgher Mil.; yúl-pa-rnams (yul pa rnams) the people, the public Mil. — yúl-po (yul po) gen. with c̀e (che), a large country, Mil. — yúl-dpon (yul dpon) village magistrate, district judge. — yul-p̱yógs (yul phyogs) region, neighbourhood Mil. — yúl-ma (yul ma) a native woman. — yúl-mi (yul mi) 1. = yúl-pa (yul pa). 2. countryman, compatriot Do. — yul-méd (yul med) 1. improper, not in its place Cs. 2. ráṅ-snaṅ yul-méd bstán-du gsol (rang snang yul med bstan du gsol) Glr. was explained: what has no place in my mind, what I do not know or understand, I beg you to teach me. — yúl-tso (yul tsho) village, borough, = gróṅ-tso (grong tsho). — *yul-tsód-zum-ḵan* (*yul tshod zum mkhan*) W. land-surveyor, engineer. — yul-gzís (yul gzhis) v. gzís (gzhis). — yul-bzáṅ (yul bzang) fair weather Cs., yet cf. yul-ṅán (yul ngan). — yúl-yod-pa (yul yod pa) = yúl-c̀an (yul can) Cs. — yúl-len (yul len) the mode of forwarding letters from village to village, instead of expediting them in longer and regular stages. — yul-bśád (yul bshad) geography or topography. — yul-sá (yul sa) dwelling-place, habitation W. — yul-sríd (yul srid) government of a country Schr. — yul-sréd (yul sred) = yul-la ꜝdód-pa (yul la 'dod pa) attachment to one's native place, the love of country and of home, Mil.
less corr. spelling for nyúl-ba (nyul ba) Tar.

1. boasting, bragging, puff, yus c̀e don c̀uṅ (yus che don chung) Mil. much bragging, and nothing in it, yus c̀e śes c̀uṅ (yus che shes chung) Mil. one that boasts much, and knows very little; yus brjod-pa (yus brjod pa), byed-pa (byed pa) to boast Cs. — 2. pride, ḵoṅ yus ma c̀e źig (khong yus ma che zhig) do not take too much pride in your heart Mil.; lás-la byas yus c̀é-na ṅó-so c̀uṅ (las la byas yus che na ngo so chung) the more a man is pleased with himself after his deed, the less (real) happiness. — 3. blame, charge, accusation Schr. (?), false accusation Sch. (?), yus byéd-pa (yus byed pa) to charge, accuse Schr. — 4. ardour, fervour, transport, dád-pai yús-kyis (dad pa'i yus kyis) in the fervour of devotion, e.g. to shed tears, to fall down on the ground Pth. — 5. yus ꜝtúd-pa (yus 'thud pa) to fasten one cord to another, to knit or join things together Sch.

1. Cs.: 'yé-ma (ye ma) beginning and eternity, ye-ldán (ye ldan) eternal'. This word is known to me only as an adv., completely, perfectly, highly, quite; yé-nas (ye nas) id.; ye-dág (ye dag) quite clean, ye-rdzógs (ye rdzogs) quite perfect, yé-nas bzáṅ-po (ye nas bzang po) altogether good; with a negative following, not at all, ye ma ꜝdod (ye ma 'dod) I felt no inclination at all, ye ma źíg-par ꜝdug (ye ma zhig par 'dug) Mil. he was not hurt at all, yé-nas mi byed dgos (ye nas mi byed dgos) that is not to be done by any means; ye-śès (ye shes) (vulgo Ld. *"i-śes"* (*i shes*)) ज्ञान, the perfect, absolute, heavenly, divine wisdom; less frq. resp. ye-mkyéṅ (ye mkhyen); ye-śès lṅa (ye shes lnga) the five kinds of divine wisdom, of which, acc. to some, every Buddha is possessed, acc. to others, only Adibuddha; ye-śès (ye shes), in a great measure at least, is inherent
